2009-04-01.title: Russia: Putin Foe Unrepentant

2009-04-27.abstract: The operation killed 30 militants and one paramilitary soldier, Pakistan’s Interior Ministry said.

2009-04-27.title: After 43 Years, a French Town’s Nostalgia for Harry and Joe Lingers
2009-04-27.abstract: American influence in a town with a NATO base stretched culinary, military, social and sartorial boundaries.

2009-04-27.title: As Economic Turmoil Mounts, So Do Attacks on Hungary’s Gypsies
2009-04-27.abstract: Over the past year, at least seven Roma — long among Europe’s most oppressed minority groups — have been killed in Hungary.

2009-04-27.title: Ecuador Re-elects President, Preliminary Results Show
2009-04-27.abstract: The victory further cements the power of President Rafael Correa, an American-educated economist first elected in 2006.

2009-04-28.title: China Still Presses Crusade Against Falun Gong
2009-04-28.abstract: The repression of a spiritual group continues, with perhaps 8,000 practitioners detained in the past year — some of them fatally.

2009-04-28.title: China: Verdict for Abbot Postponed
2009-04-28.abstract: A Chinese court has indefinitely postponed delivering a verdict in the case of a Tibetan abbot charged with illegal possession of weapons and embezzlement, a lawyer for the abbot said Monday.

2009-04-28.title: Russia: Officer Kills 3, Police Say
2009-04-28.abstract: The head of a Moscow district police department went on a shooting rampage at a supermarket just after midnight on Monday, killing three people and wounding at least six others.

2009-04-28.title: Islamists Threaten to Kill Hostage
2009-04-28.abstract: A group claiming to be Al Qaeda’s North African wing has threatened to kill a British hostage in 20 days unless the British government releases Abu Qatada.

2009-04-28.title: Plot Foiled? In Bolivia, Truth Is Elusive
2009-04-28.abstract: The police kill three men portrayed as would-be assassins, but questions follow about what happened and what the men were really up to.

2009-04-29.title: Shadows of Violence Cling to Indian Politician
2009-04-29.abstract: The state has launched an investigation into the role of Narendra Modi, chief minister of Gujarat State, in an alleged conspiracy to attack Muslims.

2009-04-29.title: A Gift Bowl Serves Victories to a Village
2009-04-29.abstract: Where dragon boat racing is concerned, a legend that began in 1961 has cloaked Mannao, China, with an aura of invincibility.

2009-04-29.title: Palestinian Rivals to Try Once More for an Accord
2009-04-29.abstract: After four rounds of reconciliation talks, Fatah and Hamas said they would try meeting one more time.

2009-04-29.title: Poppies a Target in Fight Against Taliban
2009-04-29.abstract: U.S. commanders hope a troop increase can help cut off the multimillion-dollar opium crop that funds the Taliban.

2009-04-29.title: From Édgar, 5, Coughs Heard Round the World
2009-04-29.abstract: The Mexican government has identified a young boy as the first person in the country infected with swine flu, but officials have been slow to stem its spread.

2009-04-30.title: Containing Flu Is Not Feasible, Specialists Say
2009-04-30.abstract: Closing borders or restricting travel to thwart a flu virus would provide a marginal benefit at a very heavy economic cost, analysts say.

2009-04-30.title: Baghdad Is Shaken by a Series of Bombs
2009-04-30.abstract: So far in April, at least 300 Iraqis have been killed in bombing attacks, making it the bloodiest month since the start of the year.

2009-04-30.title: Credentials Challenged, Radical Quotes West Point
2009-04-30.abstract: Abu Muhammad al-Maqdisi, a theorist of jihad, defended his hard-line credentials by citing Western analysts.

2009-04-30.title: Premier’s Roving Eye Enrages Wife, but Not His Public
2009-04-30.abstract: The wife of Italian Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi has again publicly rebuked him for consorting with young women, a kind of soap opera that has gripped the country.

2009-04-30.title: Former S. Korean President Apologizes for Scandal
2009-04-30.abstract: Former President Roh Moo-hyun of South Korea apologized before questioning by state prosecutors in a corruption scandal that has already landed some of his relatives and aides in jail.
